revision.text: #redirect[[en:Takakura_family]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''高倉家（たかくらけ）'''は[[藤原北家]]である[[藤原長良]]の子孫である[[藤原範賢]]の子[[高倉家|高倉永季]]（[[位階|従二位]]・[[参議]]）（[[1338年]]～[[1392年]]）を祖とする[[堂上]]家である。
[[長良]]の系統は[[藤原北家]]の主流から大きく外れ、[[高倉家|永季]]の父[[藤原範賢]]までは、[[六位蔵人]]を務める[[地下]]官人だった。しかし[[高倉家|長季]]は[[衣文道]]を以って[[後光厳天皇]]・[[後円融天皇]]・[[後小松天皇]]の３代にわたって仕え、その功により[[公卿]]の仲間入りを果たした。
[[高倉家|永季]]子の[[高倉家|永行]]（[[位階|正三位]]・[[参議]]）（生年不詳～[[1416年]]）以降は、[[足利将軍家]]の[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[朝廷]]での[[有職故実]]を[[山科家]]にも伝授した。これが[[衣文道]]の[[高倉流]]・[[山科流]]の始まりである。
[[戦国時代]]の[[高倉家|高倉永相]]（[[1530年]]～[[1585年]]）は最後の[[室町幕府]][[将軍]]・[[足利義昭]]とともに[[二条城]]で[[織田信長]]と対決したが敗北し、[[織田信長|信長]]に投降した。そして後に許されて[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]となり、これ以降の[[高倉]]家の当主の[[極官]]となる。
江戸時代は代々[[徳川将軍家]]の衣装調達や[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[江戸]]滞在の折には各大名家で[[有職故実]]や[[衣文道]]の知識を広めていった。
家格は[[半家]]で[[極官]]は[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]。
[[江戸時代]]の家禄は812石、[[明治時代]]以降は[[子爵]]。
高倉家伝来の古文書・典籍・装束などは[[高倉文化研究所]]に所蔵されており、同研究所は伝統保持のための運動を地道に進めている。
== 関連事項 == *[[藤原北家]] *[[藤原長良]] *[[半家]] *[[有職故実]] *[[衣文道]] *[[山科家]] *[[室町幕府]] *[[江戸幕府]] {{History-stub}} [[Category:日本の氏族|ふしわらし]] [[category:貴族|たかくらけ]]
[[en:Fujiwara_clan]]

'''高倉家（たかくらけ）'''は[[藤原北家]]である[[藤原長良]]の子孫である[[藤原範賢]]の子[[高倉家|高倉永季]]（ながすえ）（[[位階|従二位]]・[[参議]]）（[[1338年]]～[[1392年]]）を祖とする[[堂上]]家である。
[[長良]]の系統は[[藤原北家]]の主流から大きく外れ、[[高倉家|永季]]の父[[藤原範賢]]までは、[[六位蔵人]]を務める[[地下]]官人だった。しかし[[高倉家|長季]]（ながすえ）は[[衣文道]]を以って[[後光厳天皇]]・[[後円融天皇]]・[[後小松天皇]]の３代にわたって仕え、その功により[[公卿]]の仲間入りを果たした。
[[高倉家|永季]]子の[[高倉家|永行]]（[[位階|正三位]]・[[参議]]）（生年不詳～[[1416年]]）以降は、[[足利将軍家]]の[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[朝廷]]での[[有職故実]]を[[山科家]]にも伝授した。これが[[衣文道]]の[[高倉流]]・[[山科流]]の始まりである。
[[戦国時代]]の[[高倉家|高倉永相]]（ながすけ）（[[1530年]]～[[1585年]]）は最後の[[室町幕府]][[将軍]]・[[足利義昭]]とともに[[二条城]]で[[織田信長]]と対決したが敗北し、[[織田信長|信長]]に投降した。そして後に許されて[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]となり、これ以降の[[高倉]]家の当主の[[極官]]となる。
江戸時代は代々[[徳川将軍家]]の衣装調達や[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[江戸]]滞在の折には各大名家で[[有職故実]]や[[衣文道]]の知識を広めていった。
家格は[[半家]]で[[極官]]は[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]。
[[江戸時代]]の家禄は812石、[[明治時代]]以降は[[子爵]]。
高倉家伝来の古文書・典籍・装束などは[[高倉文化研究所]]に所蔵されており、同研究所は伝統保持のための運動を地道に進めている。
== 関連事項 == *[[藤原北家]] *[[藤原長良]] *[[半家]] *[[有職故実]] *[[衣文道]] *[[山科家]] *[[室町幕府]] *[[江戸幕府]] {{History-stub}} [[Category:日本の氏族|ふしわらし]] [[category:貴族|たかくらけ]]
[[en:Fujiwara_clan]]

revision.comment: 戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代
revision.text:
'''高倉家（たかくらけ）'''は[[藤原北家]]である[[藤原長良]]の子孫である[[藤原範賢]]の子'''[[高倉永季]]'''（ながすえ）（[[位階|従二位]]・[[参議]]）（[[1338年]] - [[1392年]]）を祖とする[[堂上家]]である。
長良の系統は[[藤原北家]]の主流から大きく外れ、永季の父[[藤原範賢]]までは[[六位蔵人]]を務める[[地下]]官人だった。しかし[[高倉永季]]（ながすえ）は[[衣文道]]を以って[[後光厳天皇]]・[[後円融天皇]]・[[後小松天皇]]の3代にわたって仕え、その功により[[公卿]]の仲間入りを果たした。
[[高倉永季]]の子の[[高倉永行|永行]]（[[位階|正三位]]・[[参議]]）（生年不詳 - [[1416年]]）以降は[[足利将軍家]]の[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[朝廷]]での[[有職故実]]を[[山科家]]にも伝授した。これが[[衣文道]]の[[高倉流]]・[[山科流]]の始まりである。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[高倉永相]]（ながすけ）（[[1530年]] - [[1585年]]）は最後の[[室町幕府]][[将軍]]・[[足利義昭]]とともに[[二条城]]で[[織田信長]]と対決したが敗北し、信長に投降した。そして後に許されて[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]となり、これ以降の[[高倉家]]の当主の[[極官]]となる。
江戸時代は代々[[徳川将軍家]]の衣装調達や[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[江戸]]滞在の折には各大名家で[[有職故実]]や[[衣文道]]の知識を広めていった。
家格は[[半家]]で[[極官]]は[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]。

[[高倉永季]]の子の[[高倉永行|永行]]（[[位階|正三位]]・[[参議]]）（生年不詳 - [[1416年]]）以降は[[足利将軍家]]の[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[朝廷]]での[[有職故実]]を[[山科家]]にも伝授した。これが[[衣文道]]の[[高倉流]]・[[山科流]]の始まりである。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[高倉永相]]（ながすけ）（[[1530年]] - [[1585年]]）は最後の[[室町幕府]][[将軍]]・[[足利義昭]]とともに[[二条城]]で[[織田信長]]と対決したが敗北し、信長に投降した。そして後に許されて[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]となり、これ以降の[[高倉家]]の当主の[[極官]]となる。
江戸時代は代々[[徳川将軍家]]の衣装調達や[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[江戸]]滞在の折には各大名家で[[有職故実]]や[[衣文道]]の知識を広めていった。
家格は[[半家]]で[[極官]]は[[位階|正二位]]・[[権大納言]]。
[[江戸時代]]の家禄は812石、[[明治時代]]以降は[[子爵]]。
高倉家伝来の古文書・典籍・装束などは、現在[http://www011.upp.so-net.ne.jp/yuusoku/top.html 有職文化研究所]（旧名「高倉文化研究所」）に所蔵されており、同研究所は伝統保持のための運動を地道に進めている。

[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[高倉永相]]（ながすけ）（[[1530年]] - [[1585年]]）は最後の[[室町幕府]][[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[足利義昭]]とともに[[二条城]]で[[織田信長]]と対決したが敗北し、信長に投降した。そして後に許されて[[正二位]]・[[権大納言]]となり、これ以降の[[高倉家]]の当主の[[極官]]となる。
江戸時代は代々[[徳川将軍家]]の衣装調達や[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[江戸]]滞在の折には各大名家で[[有職故実]]や[[衣文道]]の知識を広めていった。また、江戸時代の分家として[[堀河家]]・[[樋口家]]の両[[羽林家]]がある。
[[江戸時代]]の家禄は812石、[[明治時代]]以降は[[子爵]]。
高倉家伝来の古文書・典籍・装束などは、現在[http://yuusoku.jp/ 有職文化研究所]（旧名「高倉文化研究所」）に所蔵されており、同研究所は伝統保持のための運動を地道に進めている。
== 関連項目 == * [[藤原北家]] * [[藤原長良]] * [[半家 (公家)|半家]] * [[有職故実]] * [[衣文道]] * [[山科家]] * [[室町幕府]] * [[江戸幕府]]

[[樋口家]]（[[羽林家]]） }} '''高倉家'''（たかくらけ）は[[藤原北家]]である[[藤原長良]]の子孫である[[藤原範賢]]の子'''[[高倉永季]]'''（ながすえ）（[[従二位]]・[[参議]]）（[[1338年]] - [[1392年]]）を祖とする[[半家 (公家)|半家]]の[[家格]]を持つ[[堂上家]]。 == 概要 == 長良の系統は[[藤原北家]]の主流から大きく外れ、永季の父[[藤原範賢]]までは[[六位蔵人]]を務める[[地下人|地下官人]]だった。しかし[[高倉永季]]（ながすえ）は[[衣文道]]を以って[[後光厳天皇]]・[[後円融天皇]]・[[後小松天皇]]の3代にわたって仕え、その功により[[公卿]]の仲間入りを果たした。 [[高倉永季]]の子の[[高倉永行|永行]]（[[正三位]]・[[参議]]）（生年不詳 - [[1416年]]）以降は[[足利将軍家]]の[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[朝廷]]での[[有職故実]]を[[山科家]]にも伝授した。これが[[衣文道]]の[[高倉流]]・[[山科流]]の始まりである。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[高倉永相]]（ながすけ）（[[1530年]] - [[1585年]]）は最後の[[室町幕府]][[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[足利義昭]]とともに[[二条城]]で[[織田信長]]と対決したが敗北し、信長に投降した。そして後に許されて[[正二位]]・[[大納言|権大納言]]となり、これ以降の[[高倉家]]の当主の[[極官]]となる。 江戸時代は代々[[徳川将軍家]]の衣装調達や[[衣文道]]の指導にも当たり、[[江戸]]滞在の折には各大名家で[[有職故実]]や[[衣文道]]の知識を広めていった。また、[[江戸時代]]の分家として[[堀河家]]・[[樋口家]]の両[[羽林家]]がある。 [[江戸時代]]の家禄は812石、[[明治時代]]以降は[[子爵]]。 高倉家伝来の古文書・典籍・装束などは、現在[http://yuusoku.jp/ 有職文化研究所]（旧名「高倉文化研究所」）に所蔵されており、同研究所は伝統保持のための運動を地道に進めている。 == 関連項目 == * [[藤原北家]] * [[藤原長良]] * [[半家 (公家)|半家]] * [[有職故実]] * [[衣文道]] * [[山科家]] * [[室町幕府]] * [[江戸幕府]] == 外部リンク　== * [http://yuusoku.jp/ 有職文化研究所] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらけ}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:藤原北家|+]] [[category:公家]] [[en:Fujiwara_clan]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[Category:Half-House (Kuge)]] [[Category:Japanese Viscount Families]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takakura family]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takakura family]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takakura family}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高倉家]]

This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[Category:Half-House (Kuge)]] [[Category:Japanese Viscount Families]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takakura family]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takakura family]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takakura family}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高倉家]]

{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[Category:Half-House (Kuge)]] [[Category:Japanese Viscount Families]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takakura family]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takakura family]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takakura family}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takakura family]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高倉家]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - {{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - {{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - {{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nanke|ja|藤原南家}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}} {{ill|Nagano Lineage|ja|藤原長良}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}})
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2年）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990年（平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18年）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }} {{ill|Heian-kyo|ja|平安京}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}}・{{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hokke|ja|藤原北家}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}}, a {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|ja|公家|1=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara Nagano|ja|藤原長良}}, a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} elevated the family name by attaining the rank of {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|ja|菩提寺}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - [[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|qq=|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|qq=|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|qq=|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|qq=|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|qq=|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|qq=|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|qq=|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|qq=|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|qq=|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|qq=|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|qq=|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|qq=|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}}

Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|qq=|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}}

[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}}
