[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] [[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] [[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] [[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''[[:en:Fujiwara Nan-ke|Fujiwara Nanke]]''' of [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Nagano Lineage]] |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] ([[:en:Viscount|Viscount]]) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyo]] |residence = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・[[:en:Viscount|Viscount]])}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] lineage of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Hokke]], tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]], their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, they were ennobled as [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagara|Fujiwara Nagano]], a son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu]], and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] and [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their [[:en:Bodaiji|Bodaiji]] was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]], serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the [[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of [[:en:Viscount|Viscount]] {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:高倉家]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] [[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] [[ja:高倉家]][[ko:다카쿠라가]][[zh:高倉家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668764]] Wikidata name: Takakura family [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruseslist|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagara|en|Fujiwara no Nagara|WD=Q667119|lt=Nagano Lineage}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}) and {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=Kazoku}} ({{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})|'''{{ill|Fujiwara Nan-ke|en|Fujiwara Nan-ke|WD=Q2975332|lt=Fujiwara Nanke}}''' of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} ({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}})|{{ill|Yabu Family|ja|藪家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Family|1=Yabu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869576}}|High-Rank Samurai of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}|{{ill|Nihonmatsu Clan|ja|二本松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonmatsu Clan|1=Nihonmatsu Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371632}}}} {{Infobox Japanese family |family_name = Takakura Family |family_crest = Sasa Rindō inverted.png |crest_caption = {{ruby|Sasa Rindō|笹竜胆}} |lineage = {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagara|en|Fujiwara no Nagara|WD=Q667119|lt=Nagano Lineage}} |founder = {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} |type = {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} ({{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}})
{{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=Kazoku}} ({{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount}}) |origin = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyo}} |residence = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} |branches = {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}} {{small|({{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}}・{{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount}})}} }} '''Takakura Family''' (高倉家) refers to a {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}} lineage of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke}}, tracing their descent from {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}}, a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}} and holder of {{ill|Junior Second Rank|ja|従二位|1=Junior Second Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Second Rank|qq=}}. As a {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge}}, their status was classified as {{ill|Half-House|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Half-House|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=Kazoku}} system, they were ennobled as {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount}} {{Sfn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. Their branch family, the {{ill|Horikawa Family|ja|堀河家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horikawa Family|1=Horikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427578}}, also belonged to the {{ill|Feather-Forest House|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feather-Forest House|qq=}} and held the {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount}} title {{sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=413}}. == History == The family originated with {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagara|en|Fujiwara no Nagara|WD=Q667119|lt=Fujiwara Nagano}}, a son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|en|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|WD=Q3090616|lt=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu}}, and developed through his sixth son {{ill|Fujiwara Kiyotsune|ja|藤原清経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Kiyotsune|1=Fujiwara no Kiyotsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623384}} {{Sfn|Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|1990|p=164}}. Over the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} and {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, few family members achieved {{ill|Kugyō|1=kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3138036}} Rank{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉家|encyclopedia=世界大百科事典 第２版、日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ)|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} elevated the family name by attaining the Rank of {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}, establishing the Takakura family line. The family name "Takakura" derives from their residence in the Takakura district of Kyoto. Their household responsibilities included overseeing court garments and the hereditary {{ill|Emondō|ja|衣紋道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emondō|qq=}} tradition . During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the Takakura family possessed a stipend of 812 koku, with lands in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Their main residence was located in Nikaimachi, and their {{ill|Bodaiji|en|Bodaiji|WD=Q1084697|lt=Bodaiji}} was the Jōgen-in temple at Kurodani {{SFn|Ota Ryo|1934|p=3274}}. === Bakumatsu Period and Meiji Restoration === In the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu}} period, the head of the family, {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}}, played a significant role in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, serving as a general in various campaigns. However, he died in 1868 in Echigo Province during the war{{Cite Kotobank|word=高倉永祜|encyclopedia=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus|accessdate=2022-11-16}}. In 1869, under the new {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=Kazoku}} system, the Takakura family was ennobled as part of the {{ill|Kazoku|en|Kazoku|WD=Q241734|lt=Kazoku}} hierarchy. In 1884, {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}}, a former military officer, was granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|WD=Q185902|lt=Viscount}} {{sfn|Odabe Yuji|2006|p=332}}. == Notable Members == - {{ill|Takakura Nagasue|ja|高倉永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagasue|1=Takakura Nagasue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q102246579}} - {{ill|Takakura Nagayoshi|ja|高倉永祜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Nagayoshi|1=Takakura Nagayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226013}} - {{ill|Takakura Naganori|ja|高倉永則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura Naganori|1=Takakura Nagatsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470190}} == Related Institutions == The family archives, including historical documents, texts, and ceremonial garments, are preserved by the Yūsoku Cultural Institute (formerly the Takakura Cultural Institute). This organization is committed to preserving the traditions associated with the Takakura lineage. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == - {{Cite book|和書|date=1990th year （平成2nd year ）|title=華族大鑑|series=日本人物誌叢書7|author=Kazoku Daikan Kanko Kai|publisher=[[Nihon Toshokan Center]]|isbn=978-4820540342}} - {{Cite book|和書|date=2006年（平成18th year ）|title=華族 近代日本貴族の虚像と実像|author=Odabe Yuji|authorlink=小田部雄次|publisher=[[Chuo Koron Shinsha]]|series=[[Chuko Shinsho]]1836|isbn=978-4121018366}} == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/okugesan_com/hanke2.htm#takakura |title=Kuge Family Genealogies: Takakura |date=20190101000000}} * [https://reichsarchiv.jp/%e5%ae%b6%e7%b3%bb%e3%83%aa%e3%82%b9%e3%83%88/%e9%ab%98%e5%80%89%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%88%e5%8d%8a%e5%ae%b6%ef%bc%89 Takakura Family (Half-House)] - Reichsarchiv {{translated page|ja|高倉家|version=103136002|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高倉家]] [[ko:다카쿠라가]] {{wikidata link|Q11668764}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takakura Family]] [[Category:Viscount Families of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
